title: Mild Dense Korean Pork Shoulder Mandu
description: Spicy, dense, and cozy Korean dish from Jeju featuring pork shoulder steaks, roast, and mandu dumplings, perfect for a weeknight dinner, easy to make and fun to eat, even raw-friendly!
instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend the onion, garlic, soy sauce, Gochujang, brown sugar, sesame oil, ginger, and black pepper until smooth.
Place the pork shoulder steaks in a large bowl and marinate them in the blended mixture for at least 30 minutes.

Place the marinated pork shoulder steaks on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until browned on both sides and cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Let the pork shoulder steaks rest for 5 minutes before slicing them into thin strips.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced pork shoulder steaks, kimchi, and diced onion, and mix well.
Place a mandu wrapper on a clean surface and brush the edges with vegetable oil.
Place a small spoonful of the pork and kimchi mixture in the center of the wrapper and brush the edges with the beaten egg.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle and press the edges together to seal the mandu dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling, until all the ingredients have been used up.
In a large pot or deep fryer, heat about 2-3 inches of vegetable oil to 375°F (190°C).
When the oil is hot, add a few of the mandu dumplings to the pot and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Repeat with the remaining mandu dumplings, until all of them have been fried.
Drain the mandu dumplings on paper towels and serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
